---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: c8bbb4bc18d8d785aacd0f43ee4874a2f65df36a
findings:
  - { id: judged-frontier-refusal-request-channel, kind: judged, text: "ac-2's frontier refusal ('a request for capability outside that frontier … is refused … and the interview continues') is implemented as a fixed, always-asked yes/no probe question that every interview poses ('Add, remove, or restructure the class hierarchy…?', internal/initwizard/interview.go:104) rather than as a response to an operator-initiated request. This satisfies the refuse-and-continue behavior when answered 'yes', but it means the interview surface is not 'exactly the v1 frontier's two configurable axes' — a third, out-of-frontier solicitation is part of every run and every stdin script must answer it — and a structural request expressed anywhere else (e.g. typed as free text at a rename prompt) is silently accepted as a display word, never refused with the frontier explanation. (confidence 0.40)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "A deterministic CLI cannot detect structural intent in free text; the explicit probe IS the honest v1 request channel; rename prompts accept typed text as display words by their own contract." }
  - { id: judged-declined-write-exit-code, kind: judged, text: "The wizard still poses a final 'Write .verdi/ ?' confirmation (internal/initwizard/interview.go:117-123) that no AC describes — ac-2 scopes the interview to the two configurable axes, and ac-3's abort taxonomy covers only stdin-ends-early and staging crashes. The earlier exit-code half of this finding is resolved: a deliberate decline (ErrDeclinedWrite) now exits 0 with a plain stdout line and the staged temp discarded (cmd/verdi/init.go:142-145), a defensible 'clean no-op' classification under the module's 0/1/2 discipline. What remains is the unauthorized prompt itself plus a whole decline outcome (exit 0, 'nothing was written') that exists outside every AC, and which any stdin script written from the spec alone would fail to answer, hitting ErrAborted instead. (confidence 0.25)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Write confirmation kept-and-disclosed (spec-body Note); decline is a clean exit-0 no-op — judge-acknowledged defensible under the 0/1/2 discipline; the prompt is a disclosed interview-flow addition beyond the AC letter." }
  - { id: judged-crash-injection-fidelity, kind: judged, text: "ac-3's 'simulated crash injected partway through staging' is implemented as an ordinary error return (simulateCrashAfter, cmd/verdi/init.go:338-343) that still executes the deferred os.RemoveAll(tempRoot) — so the built-binary evidence proves the error-path cleanup, not survival of an actual process death, under which the deferred discard never runs and a populated .verdi-init-tmp-* wrapper persists beside the target. The load-bearing invariant (no .verdi/ ever appears at the real root) does still hold by construction since no real-root write precedes the single rename, and the AC's own wording attributes the guarantee to discard-on-error, so this is a fidelity gap in what the crash pin demonstrates rather than a violated outcome. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "The pin proves discard-on-error — exactly what the AC letter attributes; true process death leaves at most an inert sibling temp dir, never a partial .verdi, by construction (no real-root write precedes the single rename)." }
  - { id: judged-promotion-rename-mechanism, kind: judged, text: "The empty-.verdi silent-replace gap previously reported as existing-verdi-enotempty-backstop is closed at this head: promotion now goes through renameExclusive (cmd/verdi/init.go:210-223; darwin renamex_np/RENAME_EXCL, linux renameat2/RENAME_NOREPLACE), which refuses ANY existing destination, empty directory included. But the spec's own promotion story now lags the implementation in three particulars: ac-3 says promotion 'is exactly one os.Rename' while the actual call is a platform-specific rename-exclusive syscall (still exactly one rename, but not os.Rename, and semantically stronger); ac-1 attributes the backstop failure to ENOTEMPTY while the implemented backstop fails with an os.ErrExist/EEXIST-classified error, and precisely in the empty-directory case ENOTEMPTY never occurs — the AC's stated mechanism is not the one that protects; and the !darwin \u0026\u0026 !linux arm (init_rename_unsupported.go) makes verdi init refuse to promote entirely on any other GOOS, a platform-support restriction no AC or spec section discloses. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Implementation deliberately stronger than the frozen letter: promotion is exactly one atomic rename-exclusive syscall (EEXIST on ANY existing destination) rather than bare os.Rename+ENOTEMPTY; mechanism, platform arms, and unsupported-GOOS refusal disclosed in the spec-body Note. Frozen-text corrections (ac-1 ENOTEMPTY attribution, ac-3 'os.Rename' letter, platform-support disclosure) queued for the feature-close ratification pass." }
  - { id: judged-preview-every-answer, kind: judged, text: "ac-2 says 'every answer is previewed live by validating the in-progress candidate model in memory … before the interview moves on', but runRenamePrompt (internal/initwizard/interview.go:142-171) validates and previews only non-empty rename answers: an Enter-to-keep answer returns immediately with no validation or preview line, and the template-copy and structural-probe yes/no answers are never previewed against the candidate model at all. The AC's underlying intent — no answer mutates the candidate without passing the same kernel+frontier validation model check runs — does hold, since only validated answers are applied and DecodeModel runs Validate plus checkFrontier (internal/model/decode.go:37-42); the deviation is confined to the literal 'every answer' claim. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "'Every answer' read as every answer that mutates the candidate: Enter-to-keep mutates nothing, probe answers are not model values; the no-unvalidated-mutation intent is proven (only DecodeModel-validated answers apply)." }
digest: sha256:7d6c28e1c0ca76bd069e6ce3abb4d46d9b4eebc595121e025b1ba0941bdda13f
integrity: sha256:89962241ac86aae249cb13c2d8b2f6d313ee5817ef27a4c84e8671e1a8392037
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"frontier-refusal-request-channel\",\"text\":\"ac-2's frontier refusal ('a request for capability outside that frontier … is refused … and the interview continues') is implemented as a fixed, always-asked yes/no probe question that every interview poses ('Add, remove, or restructure the class hierarchy…?', internal/initwizard/interview.go:104) rather than as a response to an operator-initiated request. This satisfies the refuse-and-continue behavior when answered 'yes', but it means the interview surface is not 'exactly the v1 frontier's two configurable axes' — a third, out-of-frontier solicitation is part of every run and every stdin script must answer it — and a structural request expressed anywhere else (e.g. typed as free text at a rename prompt) is silently accepted as a display word, never refused with the frontier explanation.\",\"confidence\":0.4},{\"id\":\"declined-write-exit-code\",\"text\":\"The wizard still poses a final 'Write .verdi/ ?' confirmation (internal/initwizard/interview.go:117-123) that no AC describes — ac-2 scopes the interview to the two configurable axes, and ac-3's abort taxonomy covers only stdin-ends-early and staging crashes. The earlier exit-code half of this finding is resolved: a deliberate decline (ErrDeclinedWrite) now exits 0 with a plain stdout line and the staged temp discarded (cmd/verdi/init.go:142-145), a defensible 'clean no-op' classification under the module's 0/1/2 discipline. What remains is the unauthorized prompt itself plus a whole decline outcome (exit 0, 'nothing was written') that exists outside every AC, and which any stdin script written from the spec alone would fail to answer, hitting ErrAborted instead.\",\"confidence\":0.25},{\"id\":\"crash-injection-fidelity\",\"text\":\"ac-3's 'simulated crash injected partway through staging' is implemented as an ordinary error return (simulateCrashAfter, cmd/verdi/init.go:338-343) that still executes the deferred os.RemoveAll(tempRoot) — so the built-binary evidence proves the error-path cleanup, not survival of an actual process death, under which the deferred discard never runs and a populated .verdi-init-tmp-* wrapper persists beside the target. The load-bearing invariant (no .verdi/ ever appears at the real root) does still hold by construction since no real-root write precedes the single rename, and the AC's own wording attributes the guarantee to discard-on-error, so this is a fidelity gap in what the crash pin demonstrates rather than a violated outcome.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"promotion-rename-mechanism\",\"text\":\"The empty-.verdi silent-replace gap previously reported as existing-verdi-enotempty-backstop is closed at this head: promotion now goes through renameExclusive (cmd/verdi/init.go:210-223; darwin renamex_np/RENAME_EXCL, linux renameat2/RENAME_NOREPLACE), which refuses ANY existing destination, empty directory included. But the spec's own promotion story now lags the implementation in three particulars: ac-3 says promotion 'is exactly one os.Rename' while the actual call is a platform-specific rename-exclusive syscall (still exactly one rename, but not os.Rename, and semantically stronger); ac-1 attributes the backstop failure to ENOTEMPTY while the implemented backstop fails with an os.ErrExist/EEXIST-classified error, and precisely in the empty-directory case ENOTEMPTY never occurs — the AC's stated mechanism is not the one that protects; and the !darwin \u0026\u0026 !linux arm (init_rename_unsupported.go) makes verdi init refuse to promote entirely on any other GOOS, a platform-support restriction no AC or spec section discloses.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"preview-every-answer\",\"text\":\"ac-2 says 'every answer is previewed live by validating the in-progress candidate model in memory … before the interview moves on', but runRenamePrompt (internal/initwizard/interview.go:142-171) validates and previews only non-empty rename answers: an Enter-to-keep answer returns immediately with no validation or preview line, and the template-copy and structural-probe yes/no answers are never previewed against the candidate model at all. The AC's underlying intent — no answer mutates the candidate without passing the same kernel+frontier validation model check runs — does hold, since only validated answers are applied and DecodeModel runs Validate plus checkFrontier (internal/model/decode.go:37-42); the deviation is confined to the literal 'every answer' claim.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/init-wizard@c8bbb4bc18d8d785aacd0f43ee4874a2f65df36a, spec/init-wizard@a28bc2178eb7f481bc30b2b234a3d5e944b9591a], digest: sha256:7d6c28e1c0ca76bd069e6ce3abb4d46d9b4eebc595121e025b1ba0941bdda13f, integrity: sha256:89962241ac86aae249cb13c2d8b2f6d313ee5817ef27a4c84e8671e1a8392037, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-frontier-refusal-request-channel** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2's frontier refusal ('a request for capability outside that frontier … is refused … and the interview continues') is implemented as a fixed, always-asked yes/no probe question that every interview poses ('Add, remove, or restructure the class hierarchy…?', internal/initwizard/interview.go:104) rather than as a response to an operator-initiated request. This satisfies the refuse-and-continue behavior when answered 'yes', but it means the interview surface is not 'exactly the v1 frontier's two configurable axes' — a third, out-of-frontier solicitation is part of every run and every stdin script must answer it — and a structural request expressed anywhere else (e.g. typed as free text at a rename prompt) is silently accepted as a display word, never refused with the frontier explanation. (confidence 0.40) — A deterministic CLI cannot detect structural intent in free text; the explicit probe IS the honest v1 request channel; rename prompts accept typed text as display words by their own contract.
- **judged-declined-write-exit-code** [accepted-deviation]: The wizard still poses a final 'Write .verdi/ ?' confirmation (internal/initwizard/interview.go:117-123) that no AC describes — ac-2 scopes the interview to the two configurable axes, and ac-3's abort taxonomy covers only stdin-ends-early and staging crashes. The earlier exit-code half of this finding is resolved: a deliberate decline (ErrDeclinedWrite) now exits 0 with a plain stdout line and the staged temp discarded (cmd/verdi/init.go:142-145), a defensible 'clean no-op' classification under the module's 0/1/2 discipline. What remains is the unauthorized prompt itself plus a whole decline outcome (exit 0, 'nothing was written') that exists outside every AC, and which any stdin script written from the spec alone would fail to answer, hitting ErrAborted instead. (confidence 0.25) — Write confirmation kept-and-disclosed (spec-body Note); decline is a clean exit-0 no-op — judge-acknowledged defensible under the 0/1/2 discipline; the prompt is a disclosed interview-flow addition beyond the AC letter.
- **judged-crash-injection-fidelity** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3's 'simulated crash injected partway through staging' is implemented as an ordinary error return (simulateCrashAfter, cmd/verdi/init.go:338-343) that still executes the deferred os.RemoveAll(tempRoot) — so the built-binary evidence proves the error-path cleanup, not survival of an actual process death, under which the deferred discard never runs and a populated .verdi-init-tmp-* wrapper persists beside the target. The load-bearing invariant (no .verdi/ ever appears at the real root) does still hold by construction since no real-root write precedes the single rename, and the AC's own wording attributes the guarantee to discard-on-error, so this is a fidelity gap in what the crash pin demonstrates rather than a violated outcome. (confidence 0.30) — The pin proves discard-on-error — exactly what the AC letter attributes; true process death leaves at most an inert sibling temp dir, never a partial .verdi, by construction (no real-root write precedes the single rename).
- **judged-promotion-rename-mechanism** [accepted-deviation]: The empty-.verdi silent-replace gap previously reported as existing-verdi-enotempty-backstop is closed at this head: promotion now goes through renameExclusive (cmd/verdi/init.go:210-223; darwin renamex_np/RENAME_EXCL, linux renameat2/RENAME_NOREPLACE), which refuses ANY existing destination, empty directory included. But the spec's own promotion story now lags the implementation in three particulars: ac-3 says promotion 'is exactly one os.Rename' while the actual call is a platform-specific rename-exclusive syscall (still exactly one rename, but not os.Rename, and semantically stronger); ac-1 attributes the backstop failure to ENOTEMPTY while the implemented backstop fails with an os.ErrExist/EEXIST-classified error, and precisely in the empty-directory case ENOTEMPTY never occurs — the AC's stated mechanism is not the one that protects; and the !darwin && !linux arm (init_rename_unsupported.go) makes verdi init refuse to promote entirely on any other GOOS, a platform-support restriction no AC or spec section discloses. (confidence 0.35) — Implementation deliberately stronger than the frozen letter: promotion is exactly one atomic rename-exclusive syscall (EEXIST on ANY existing destination) rather than bare os.Rename+ENOTEMPTY; mechanism, platform arms, and unsupported-GOOS refusal disclosed in the spec-body Note. Frozen-text corrections (ac-1 ENOTEMPTY attribution, ac-3 'os.Rename' letter, platform-support disclosure) queued for the feature-close ratification pass.
- **judged-preview-every-answer** [accepted-deviation]: ac-2 says 'every answer is previewed live by validating the in-progress candidate model in memory … before the interview moves on', but runRenamePrompt (internal/initwizard/interview.go:142-171) validates and previews only non-empty rename answers: an Enter-to-keep answer returns immediately with no validation or preview line, and the template-copy and structural-probe yes/no answers are never previewed against the candidate model at all. The AC's underlying intent — no answer mutates the candidate without passing the same kernel+frontier validation model check runs — does hold, since only validated answers are applied and DecodeModel runs Validate plus checkFrontier (internal/model/decode.go:37-42); the deviation is confined to the literal 'every answer' claim. (confidence 0.20) — 'Every answer' read as every answer that mutates the candidate: Enter-to-keep mutates nothing, probe answers are not model values; the no-unvalidated-mutation intent is proven (only DecodeModel-validated answers apply).

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

(none)
